steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
The subject “emulsifiers such as cetyl hydroxyethylcellulose” (plural noun phrase) sits on the left of the blank.

On the right is an infinitive phrase “to blend oil and water into homogeneous formulations, like lotions and perfumes.” This positioning indicates the blank must contain the main verb of the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
An independent clause requires a finite verb that agrees with its subject in person and number.

Only are employed is a finite verb form that agrees with the plural subject “emulsifiers.” Choices B and D provide non-finite verb forms (past and present participles respectively) that cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause, and choice C introduces a relative clause that breaks the sentence structure.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
